questions.q: What is the main goal of the Gaganyaan programme?
options: To land a rover on Mars
To send Indian astronauts into orbit
To deploy a fleet of communication satellites
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan is India's programme to send astronauts to orbit aboard an indigenous spacecraft, its first human spaceflight effort.

questions.q: Which space agency is developing the Gaganyaan mission?
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan is being developed by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: What does the word 'Gaganyaan' broadly mean?
questions.explanation: 'Gaganyaan' derives from Sanskrit words meaning sky ('gagan') and vehicle/craft ('yaan').

questions.q: Gaganyaan would make India which country to independently launch humans into space?
questions.explanation: After the Soviet Union/Russia, the United States, and China, India would become the fourth nation to independently send humans to space.

questions.q: Crew members trained for human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan are generally called what?
questions.explanation: The crew selected and trained for spaceflight are astronauts; India sometimes uses the term 'Gaganyatri' for them.

questions.q: Where will the Gaganyaan crewed missions be launched from?
questions.explanation: ISRO launches its orbital missions from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: What is the target environment for the Gaganyaan crewed flight?
options: A Lagrange point near the Sun
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan is designed to carry astronauts into low Earth orbit and return them safely.

questions.q: How is the Gaganyaan crew capsule planned to return to Earth?
options: Splashdown in the sea with parachutes
questions.explanation: The Gaganyaan crew module is designed to descend under parachutes and splash down in the sea for recovery.

questions.q: From which historic landmark did the Prime Minister announce the Gaganyaan human spaceflight goal?
options: The Red Fort during an Independence Day address
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced India's human spaceflight ambition from the Red Fort during his Independence Day speech in 2018.

questions.q: Which country has Gaganyaan been a major symbol of national pride for?
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan is India's first indigenous human spaceflight programme and a milestone in its space ambitions.

questions.q: Which launch vehicle is being human-rated to carry the Gaganyaan crew to orbit?
questions.explanation: ISRO's most powerful operational rocket, the LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk III), is being human-rated for Gaganyaan.

questions.q: In which year did the Indian government formally approve significant funding for the Gaganyaan programme?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet approved the Gaganyaan programme in late 2018, the same year it was announced from the Red Fort.

questions.q: What is the name of ISRO's humanoid robot built to fly on uncrewed Gaganyaan test missions?
questions.explanation: Vyommitra is a half-humanoid robot developed by ISRO to fly aboard uncrewed Gaganyaan test flights and simulate crew functions.

questions.q: What term has ISRO used for the Indian astronauts of the Gaganyaan programme?
questions.explanation: ISRO and Indian officials have referred to the Gaganyaan crew as 'Gaganyatris' (sky travellers).

questions.q: Roughly how many crew members is the Gaganyaan capsule designed to carry on an orbital mission?
questions.explanation: The Gaganyaan crew module is designed to carry up to three astronauts to low Earth orbit.

questions.q: What is the typical planned duration of the first crewed Gaganyaan orbital mission?
questions.explanation: The planned crewed Gaganyaan mission is a short orbital flight of up to about three days before return.

questions.q: Which two main parts make up the Gaganyaan orbital module?
questions.explanation: The Gaganyaan orbital module consists of a crew module (which houses the astronauts) and a service module that supports it in orbit.

questions.q: Where were the first group of Indian astronaut-candidates for Gaganyaan given initial training before specialised preparation in India?
questions.explanation: The four shortlisted Indian Air Force pilots underwent initial astronaut training in Russia before continuing preparation in India.

questions.q: From which branch of the Indian armed forces were the first Gaganyaan astronaut-designates primarily drawn?
questions.explanation: The astronaut-designates announced for Gaganyaan were test pilots from the Indian Air Force.

questions.q: What critical safety system does Gaganyaan use to pull the crew module to safety if the rocket fails during launch?
options: A glide chute on the booster
An ejection seat for each astronaut
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan uses a Crew Escape System that can rapidly pull the crew module away from the launch vehicle in an emergency.

questions.q: What is the name of ISRO's planned crewed orbital outpost that Gaganyaan technology is meant to support in the long term?
questions.explanation: India has announced plans for the Bharatiya Antariksha Station (Indian Space Station), with Gaganyaan as a key building block.

questions.q: Before crewed flights, what kind of test missions does ISRO plan to fly under the Gaganyaan programme?
options: Crewed flights from the very first launch
Uncrewed orbital and abort test flights
questions.explanation: ISRO is conducting uncrewed test flights and in-flight abort tests to validate systems before flying humans.

questions.q: What was the name of the first in-flight abort demonstration flight (Test Vehicle Demonstration) for Gaganyaan, flown in 2023?
questions.explanation: The Test Vehicle Abort Mission-1, designated TV-D1, was successfully flown in October 2023 to demonstrate the Crew Escape System.

questions.q: Which engine, used in the LVM3's cryogenic upper stage, is part of the human-rating effort for Gaganyaan?
questions.explanation: The LVM3's upper stage uses the indigenous CE-20 cryogenic engine, which is being qualified to human-rating standards for Gaganyaan.

questions.q: What is the approximate target orbital altitude for the crewed Gaganyaan low Earth orbit mission?
questions.explanation: The Gaganyaan crewed mission is planned to operate in low Earth orbit at an altitude of roughly 400 kilometres.

questions.q: Which Indian Air Force officer was named as the designated astronaut for India's flight to the International Space Station under a related collaboration ahead of Gaganyaan?
questions.explanation: Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla, one of the Gaganyaan astronaut-designates, was assigned to fly to the ISS as part of an India-US collaboration.

questions.q: Who was the first Indian citizen to travel to space, decades before Gaganyaan, aboard a Soviet mission in 1984?
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma flew aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11 in 1984, becoming the first Indian in space, long before the indigenous Gaganyaan effort.

questions.q: What was a key purpose of the Pad Abort Test ISRO conducted in 2018 ahead of Gaganyaan?
options: To validate the crew escape system from the launch pad
To test a reusable winged spaceplane
questions.explanation: ISRO's 2018 Pad Abort Test demonstrated the crew escape system's ability to lift the crew module away from a failing rocket while still on the pad.

questions.q: Which ISRO chairman oversaw major Gaganyaan milestones including the TV-D1 abort test in 2023?
questions.explanation: S. Somanath was ISRO Chairman during the 2023 Gaganyaan test-vehicle abort demonstration and the announcement of the astronaut-designates.

questions.q: What broad category of spacecraft does the Gaganyaan crew module belong to, in terms of re-entry design?
options: A blunt-body capsule with a heat shield
questions.explanation: The Gaganyaan crew module is a blunt-body capsule that uses an ablative heat shield to survive atmospheric re-entry before parachute splashdown.
